highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.152: /code-review --fix Now Auto-Applies Suggestions
highlights.summary: The `/code-review --fix` command now automatically applies review findings directly to your working tree, surfacing reuse, simplification, and efficiency improvements without extra steps. The `/simplify` command has also been updated to invoke `/code-review --fix` under the hood. Skills and slash commands can now declare `disallowed-tools` in frontmatter, giving you finer control over what tools are available in custom workflows.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.149: Usage Breakdown, Scrollable Diffs, and Better Markdown
highlights.summary: The `/usage` command now shows a detailed per-category breakdown of what's consuming your limits, including skills, subagents, plugins, and per-MCP-server costs — making it easier to spot what's eating your quota. The `/diff` detail view now supports full keyboard navigation (arrows, `j`/`k`, `PgUp`/`PgDn`, `Space`, `Home`/`End`) so you can review changes without reaching for the mouse. Markdown output rendering has also been improved for a cleaner reading experience.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.153: Skip Git LFS and Auto-Update Notice
highlights.summary: The new `skipLfs` option for `github`/`git` plugin marketplace sources lets you skip Git LFS downloads during clone and update, which can significantly speed up plugin installs in large repos. Claude Code will now show a one-time notice if your npm global install can't auto-update, and `/doctor` will list the exact steps to fix it. Status line commands also now receive the `COLUMNS` environment variable for better terminal width awareness.

highlights.title: Claude Code as a Daily Driver: Claude.md, Skills, Subagents, Plugins, and MCPs
highlights.summary: This in-depth guide covers how to use Claude Code effectively as your primary development tool, walking through key concepts like Claude.md configuration, skills, subagents, plugins, and MCP integrations. It's a practical reference for developers who want to move beyond basic usage and build a more powerful, personalized workflow. Beginners will find it especially useful for understanding how these features work together.

highlights.title: Why Ctrl+V Won't Paste Images in Claude Code on WSL (With a Fix)
highlights.summary: If you're running Claude Code inside WSL and find that pasting images with Ctrl+V doesn't work, this post explains why — it's a clipboard bridge limitation between Windows and the Linux environment. The author walks through the specific technical reason and provides a working fix so you can paste screenshots and images directly into your Claude Code sessions. A must-read for any Windows developer using Claude Code via WSL.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest addition to the Opus model family, bringing updated capabilities that are directly accessible through Claude Code. Developers using Claude Code can switch to or evaluate Opus 4.7 for complex coding tasks where maximum reasoning power is needed. Keep an eye on your `/usage` breakdown to understand cost implications when using Opus-tier models.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial: Beginner to Advanced in 20 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial takes you from zero to productive with Claude Code in just 20 minutes, covering the essential commands and workflows you need to get started. It's an efficient watch for developers who want a structured overview without committing to a multi-hour course. Perfect for onboarding teammates or brushing up on features you may have missed.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This focused YouTube tutorial is designed to give developers a thorough understanding of Claude Code's core and advanced features within half an hour. Topics likely include slash commands, context management, and agentic workflows that help you work faster. A great middle-ground option between a quick intro and a full multi-hour course.

tips_of_the_day: Run `/doctor` after every major Claude Code update to catch any install or configuration issues early — it's like a health check that tells you exactly what to fix and how.
Use `/usage` with the new per-category breakdown to identify which skills, subagents, or MCP servers are consuming the most of your quota, then optimize or disable the ones you rarely use.
When reviewing a PR or a large diff, rely on `/diff` with keyboard navigation (`j`/`k` to scroll, `Space` to page down) to stay in the terminal and avoid context-switching to your browser or Git GUI.
